title: Built the v0 command center foundation
purpose: Create the first working version of the shared progress dashboard with Supabase schema, RLS, auth, ingest, and the local agent-log CLI.
processSummary: Built the data model and RLS policies, added server/browser/service Supabase clients, protected dashboard auth flow, implemented the /api/ingest route, and added the TypeScript CLI to post events from local work.
outcome: The project gained a working v0 architecture: Postgres schema, permissions, secure ingest path, and a CLI that could route Eric's local telemetry into the dashboard.

title: Expanded the dashboard into a usable review surface
purpose: Turn the foundation into a real dashboard David could read and comment on across projects.
processSummary: Built the main dashboard pages and components, added comments on core objects, added realtime/polling updates, documented manual Supabase setup and deployment steps, and reframed the product as a shared co-work dashboard instead of a one-way Eric view.
outcome: The product became a coherent review tool with project pages, comments, live updates, and deployment/readiness docs, while keeping read/comment-only permissions intact.

title: Added multi-project routing, labeled work, and agentic setup views
purpose: Make the dashboard useful across multiple local project directories and support labeled work beyond pure coding.
processSummary: Added the local project registry flow for multiple folders, created source setup and health views, added workType/workLabel tagging through ingest and the CLI, added session detail views, and added a manual activity composer for non-coding work.
outcome: The dashboard now supports Command Center, Dalya, Buriza, and Zaya Life as distinct projects, with filterable work labels and owner/editor manual event logging.

title: Added activity-log automation for future work capture
purpose: Make future features, fixes, bugs, reviews, strategy changes, and non-coding work easier to capture in the dashboard without hand-editing project JSON files.
processSummary: Added a TypeScript activity-log CLI that resolves the project from flags, env, or the local directory registry, appends validated records to docs/historical-activity/<projectSlug>.json, creates new project log files when needed, and can optionally trigger the Supabase historical importer.
priorIssues: Future activity capture depended on manual JSON edits
New projects needed a clear path for creating their own historical activity file
issuesIdentified: The existing agent-log cwd behavior did not account for npm --prefix usage from other project directories
fixesMade: Updated agent-log to prefer COMMAND_CENTER_WORKING_DIR or INIT_CWD before process.cwd
Added an automation-first rule for recurring workflow events
outcome: Future project work can now be captured with npm run activity-log and imported per project, while repeated event patterns are documented as candidates for hooks, watchers, or scheduled sync jobs.
